top_categories.workout_context: 170ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Marmotte Granfondo Pyrénées, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
18,701ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At mile 60 of 110, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Marmotte Granfondo Pyrénées' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before August.
18,701ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Marmotte Granfondo Pyrénées. Train it until it feels boring.
Marmotte Granfondo Pyrénées' paved Pyrenean mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 110 miles.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 110 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 18,701ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 18,701ft of climbing on paved Pyrenean mountain passes does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on paved Pyrenean mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Marmotte Granfondo Pyrénées’s 110 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Marmotte Granfondo Pyrénées. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and VO2max to prepare you for 110 miles of paved pyrenean mountain passes in Argelès-Gazost / Luz-Saint-Sauveur, Hautes-Pyrénées, France.
